name: Staurikosaurus
tagline: One of the very first dinosaurs — a nimble, sharp-toothed sprinter from the dawn of the dinosaur age.
intro: Dating from the Late Triassic period, Staurikosaurus was one of the very first dinosaurs, and one of the most primitive. It was a small, lightly built meat-eater with strong jaws and sharp teeth. Staurikosaurus's ability to run on two legs gave it an advantage over other reptiles of the period.

topics.title: Triassic Terrors
topics.text: Predators came in all shapes and sizes in prehistoric times, and not all were dinosaurs. All these hunters lived during the Triassic period, more than 200 million years ago.

captions.label: Two-legged predator
captions.text: Coelophysis had all the characteristics of a swift hunter. This ten-foot-long theropod dinosaur probably ate insects, lizards, and whatever else it could catch.

captions.label: Terror in the sky
captions.text: Eudimorphodon swooped down and snagged unsuspecting fish from the water below.

captions.label: Fish hunter
captions.text: Shonisaurus, an ichthyosaur, searched for fish in ancient seas.

captions.label: Mammal-like hunter
captions.text: Cynognathus was a member of the therapsids—a special group of reptiles thought to be precursors of mammals.

_provenance.note: Text liberated from original screen art; images & audio restored from disc. Original media is Microsoft/supplier copyright — non-commercial educational preservation.
